steps.title: Set the two expressions for y equal
steps.content:
Both equations equal $y$, so set the right sides equal:
$$x + 1 = x^2 + x$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Subtract $x$ from both sides:
$$1 = x^2$$
So $x^2 = 1$, giving $x = 1$ or $x = -1$.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• –1 –1 — one of our solutions. Check: $y = -1 + 1 = 0$ and $y = (-1)^2 + (-1) = 0$. Match. Correct.
  • 0 0 — gives $y = 1$ from the first equation but $y = 0$ from the second. Not a solution.
  • 2 2 — gives $y = 3$ vs. $y = 6$. Not a solution.
  • 3 3 — gives $y = 4$ vs. $y = 12$. Not a solution.
That is the correct answer.
